perturbation.original_text: As hereafter detailed in this Agreement, Inria via the NECS team agrees to disclose to the Organization certain proprietary and confidential traffic -related data under terms and condition s specified below to only Research and Academic use purposes without fees or other compensation, and which should not be distributed to any third parties .
perturbation.changed_text: As hereafter detailed in this Agreement, Inria via the NECS team agrees to disclose to the Individual certain proprietary and confidential traffic -related data under terms and condition s specified below to only Research and Academic use purposes without fees or other compensation, and which should not be distributed to any third parties .
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Organization" to "Individual" in Section 2 creates a contradiction because later sections still refer to "Organization," making it ambiguous whether the data disclosure applies to both or only individuals under the contact, introducing an in-text contradiction with the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The GTL Data is comprised of the GTL traffic data and information that may be provided hereunder to Organization by Inria from time to time through CSV or Database format in the NECS Team Developers Zone . The GTL Data remain the exclusive so le property of Inria. The present Agreement does not grant any transfer or assignment of commercial/exploitation/property rights on identified know -how, patents, software or any other intellectual property right held by Inria to the Organization. Promptly upon expiration or termination of this Agreement, user will cease using all GTL Data, and will return to Inria, or destroy, all originals and all copies of the GTL Data and other materials provided by Inria under this Agreement, and also certify such return or destruction in a reasonable and customary certificate provided by Inria. The obligation of confidentiality will remain until the GTL data are released to public or have fallen into public domain.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Organization,' while the changed text replaces it with 'Individual.' The contradicted text uses 'Organization' throughout, specifying obligations and rights related to the GTL data provided to the 'Organization.' By changing 'Organization' to 'Individual' in the changed text, the agreement becomes ambiguous as to whether the obligations and rights outlined in the contradicted text apply only to individuals or organizations, creating an in-text contradiction. The Perturbation Explanation clearly states that "Changing "Organization" to "Individual" in Section 2 creates a contradiction because later sections still refer to "Organization," making it ambiguous whether the data disclosure applies to both or only individuals under the contact, introducing an in-text contradiction with the contract." This satisfies the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction', specifically 'when the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.'

perturbation.original_text: Organization or individual should add below line in the acknow ledgemen t section of their publications : The authors would like to kindly acknowledge to the CNRS/INRIA NeCS team by providing traffic data from the Grenoble Traffic Lab (GTL), see Ref. [GTL]
perturbation.changed_text: The Entity should add below line in the acknow ledgemen t section of their publications : The authors would like to kindly acknowledge to the CNRS/INRIA NeCS team by providing traffic data from the Grenoble Traffic Lab (GTL), see Ref. [GTL]
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Organization or individual" to "The Entity" in Section 3A introduces a term not previously defined, and contradicts the other sections that refer to the other terms creating an in-text contradiction. It is now unclear if "Entity" refers to "User", "Recipient", "Individual", or the previously defined Organization.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Inria Contact User ( Organization) Contact Institut National de Recherche en Informatique et en Automatique (Inria) French public organism whose registered office is at Domaine de Voluceau – Rocquencourt – BP 105 – 78153 Le Chesnay Cedex Represented by its Chief Executive Officer, M. Antoine PETIT Technical Contact: Carlos Canudas de Wit CNRS, Control System Dept, GIPSA -Lab BP. 46, 38402, ST Martin d'Hères, France carlos.canudas -de-wit@gipsa -lab.fr Organizat ion: Adm Contact: Post address: Tel: Email: Technical contact: Post address: Tel: Email:
perturbation.justification: The change from "Organization or individual" to "The Entity" introduces an undefined term "The Entity." The contradicted text clearly defines "Organization" and expects this defined term to be used consistently. Replacing it with "The Entity" creates a conflict because it's now unclear who is being referred to, as the term is not defined anywhere else in the contract, thereby causing an in-text contradiction. This aligns with the Misaligned Terminology definition.

perturbation.original_text: Organization used GTL data in their paper to represent figure s, charts, table or any other experiment should add following sentence in reference to figure: “Experiment realized with traffic data provided by the NeCS team from the the Grenoble Traffic Lab (GTL), see Ref. [GTL]” Ref. [GTL]. Carlos Canudas -de-Wit, Fabio Morbidi, Luis Leon Ojeda, Alain Y. Kibangou, Iker Bellicot, Pascal Bellemain. “ Grenoble Traffic Lab: An experimental platform for advanced traffic monitoring and forecasting ” IEEE Control Systems, Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers, 2015, 35 (3), pp.23 -39.
perturbation.changed_text: The Client used GTL data in their paper to represent figure s, charts, table or any other experiment should add following sentence in reference to figure: “Experiment realized with traffic data provided by the NeCS team from the the Grenoble Traffic Lab (GTL), see Ref. [GTL]” Ref. [GTL]. Carlos Canudas -de-Wit, Fabio Morbidi, Luis Leon Ojeda, Alain Y. Kibangou, Iker Bellicot, Pascal Bellemain. “ Grenoble Traffic Lab: An experimental platform for advanced traffic monitoring and forecasting ” IEEE Control Systems, Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers, 2015, 35 (3), pp.23 -39.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Organization" to "The Client" in Section 3B introduces yet another undefined term. This creates an in-text contradiction because it's not defined whether "Client" is the same as "User", "Recipient", "Individual" or "Entity" creating ambiguity regarding the agreement's obligations and rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement will be interpreted in accordance with the laws of the France . Should a dispute arise concerning the interpretation, validity or execution of this agreement, the Parties shall bring the disput e to French Courts, which shall exclusively be competent. This agreement will only be binding when signed by both Parties in the blanks immediately below. if this document is signed first by Inria, organization will have seven (7) business days from the date of Inria’s signature below in which to return a signed pdf ver sion of this document to Inria via email to the technical contact, or this document will be deemed invalid and of no binding effect .
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Organization' which is used in the 'Contradicted Text'. By changing 'Organization' to 'The Client', and given the Perturbation Explanation that 'Client' is not defined and it is not clear if 'Client' is the same as 'User', 'Recipient', 'Individual' or 'Entity', this introduces ambiguity and therefore an in-text contradiction as it is not certain to whom the agreement applies to as indicated in the 'Contradicted Text'. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' where the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Promptly upon expiration or termination of this Agreement, user will cease using all GTL Data, and will return to Inria, or destroy, all originals and all copies of the GTL Data and other materials provided by Inria under this Agreement, and also certify such return or destruction in a reasonable and customary certificate provided by Inria.
perturbation.changed_text: Promptly upon expiration or termination of this Agreement, the Account Holder will cease using all GTL Data, and will return to Inria, or destroy, all originals and all copies of the GTL Data and other materials provided by Inria under this Agreement, and also certify such return or destruction in a reasonable and customary certificate provided by Inria.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "user" to "Account Holder" adds more confusion regarding the identity of the involved parties, especially regarding data usage after termination. It is not certain if this refers to any of the above terms. This causes an in-text contradiction because the obligations after termination are unclear: which entity must cease using the data?
perturbation.contradicted_text: This agreement , dated as of the latest date of signature below (the “Date”), is between Inria, and the person or entity /organization (Recipient) identified in the signature line below.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified that the "user" will cease using data and return it. The changed text replaces "user" with "Account Holder". The contradicted text defines the agreement as between Inria and "Recipient". The change creates an in-text contradiction because it is unclear whether the 'Account Holder' is the same as the 'Recipient' defined in the contradicted text. This ambiguity about which party has obligations under the agreement upon termination creates a direct conflict and uncertainty, because there is no definition of 'Account Holder'. Therefore the change creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This agreement will only be binding when signed by both Parties in the blanks immediately below. if this document is signed first by Inria, organization will have seven (7) business days from the date of Inria’s signature below in which to return a signed pdf ver sion of this document to Inria via email to the technical contact, or this document will be deemed invalid and of no binding effect .
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement will only be binding when signed by both Parties in the blanks immediately below. if this document is signed first by Inria, the Affiliate will have seven (7) business days from the date of Inria’s signature below in which to return a signed pdf ver sion of this document to Inria via email to the technical contact, or this document will be deemed invalid and of no binding effect .
perturbation.explanation: The change of Organization to Affiliate, further confuses the text. This is the 6th contradiction. This creates an in-text contradiction because it's not defined if "Affiliate" is a party previously defined. This is a problem because the validity depends on the correct signature.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The GTL Data is comprised of the GTL traffic data and information that may be provided hereunder to Organization by Inria from time to time through CSV or Database format in the NECS Team Developers Zone . The GTL Data remain the exclusive so le property of Inria. The present Agreement does not grant any transfer or assignment of commercial/exploitation/property rights on identified know -how, patents, software or any other intellectual property right held by Inria to the Organization. Promptly upon expiration or termination of this Agreement, the Account Holder will cease using all GTL Data, and will return to Inria, or destroy, all originals and all copies of the GTL Data and other materials provided by Inria under this Agreement, and also certify such return or destruction in a reasonable and customary certificate provided by Inria. The obligation of confidentiality will remain until the GTL data are released to public or have fallen into public domain.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Organization' to 'Affiliate' introduces uncertainty as 'Affiliate' might not be a defined party within the contract, or might be separate from 'Organization'. The contradicted text refers to 'Organization' and its obligations regarding GTL data. If 'Affiliate' is now responsible for the obligations previously assigned to 'Organization', this creates a direct conflict and ambiguity about who is bound by those obligations. The explanation notes that it is not defined if 'Affiliate' is a party previously defined. This aligns with 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' since the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.
